title: odontocarpa coronata
side_effects: Odontocarpa coronata can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems. Ingestion of the plant's leaves or flowers can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure to its oils may cause headaches and dizziness. Improper use of the plant's extracts in traditional medicine can also result in liver or kidney damage, as well as interact with prescription medications. Prolonged exposure to the plant's allergenic compounds can cause anaphylaxis, a severe and potentially life-threatening allergic reaction.
